name: Okta
shortDescription: Automate identity and access management in your Okta org from FlowRunner. Manage users, organize them into groups, automate membership with group rules, assign applications and admin roles, manage MFA factors and authenticators, read the System Log, and configure org security: sign-on and password policies with their rules, network zones, trusted origins, outbound event / inline hooks, external identity providers, profile schemas and mappings, and custom OAuth authorization servers with their token scopes, claims, access policies and rules, and signing keys. Manage application credentials end to end - SSO signing keys and CSRs, OAuth client JSON Web Keys and secrets, app logos, and resource-server keys - and identity-provider keys, signing keys, CSRs, and stored social tokens. Read and update individual application user and group assignments. Audit and revoke users' OAuth grants, tokens, and sign-in sessions. Authenticates with an Okta API token (SSWS) over your org base URL.

actions.name: Create User
actions.description: Creates a new user in your Okta org with a profile (name, email, login) and optionally a starting password. Use this to provision someone - turn off "Activate on Create" to stage the account for later activation.

actions.name: Get User
actions.description: Retrieves a single user's full profile and status by id, login, or email. Use this to read a user before updating or acting on them.

actions.name: List Users
actions.description: Lists users in your org, optionally narrowed by a quick search or a SCIM-style filter expression. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to find or enumerate users.

actions.name: Update User
actions.description: Partially updates a user's profile - only the fields you provide are changed; the rest are left as-is. Use this to edit a name, email, or phone without replacing the whole profile.

actions.name: Delete User
actions.description: Removes a user. An ACTIVE user is deactivated by the first delete; enable "Permanently Delete" to deactivate then permanently delete. Already-deactivated (DEPROVISIONED) users are handled gracefully - permanent delete works in a single step and a missing user is treated as already removed. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Get User Groups
actions.description: Lists every group a user belongs to. Use this to audit a user's group memberships.

actions.name: List Assigned App Links
actions.description: Lists the application links (apps) assigned to a user - the apps they see on their Okta dashboard. Use this to see what a user can access.

actions.name: Activate User
actions.description: Activates a STAGED or DEPROVISIONED user (status moves to ACTIVE, asynchronously). With the email off, returns an activation URL/token you can deliver yourself.

actions.name: Deactivate User
actions.description: Deactivates a user (status moves to DEPROVISIONED), removing their access. Use this to offboard someone without permanently deleting the account.

actions.name: Suspend User
actions.description: Suspends an ACTIVE user (status moves to SUSPENDED) so they cannot sign in, while keeping their assignments. Use this for a temporary hold; unsuspend to restore.

actions.name: Unsuspend User
actions.description: Returns a SUSPENDED user to ACTIVE so they can sign in again. Use this to restore access after a temporary suspension.

actions.name: Unlock User
actions.description: Unlocks a LOCKED_OUT user (returns to ACTIVE) so they can sign in with their current password. Use this when too many failed sign-ins have locked someone out.

actions.name: Expire Password
actions.description: Expires a user's password (status moves to PASSWORD_EXPIRED), forcing a reset at their next sign-in. Use this to require a password change.

actions.name: Reset Password
actions.description: Begins a password reset for a user. With the email off, returns a one-time reset URL you can deliver yourself. Use this to help a user recover access.

actions.name: Reset Factors
actions.description: Resets (clears) all enrolled MFA factors for a user, forcing re-enrollment. Use this when a user loses their device or you suspect their MFA is compromised.

actions.name: Change Password
actions.description: Changes a user's password when the current password is known (self-service style). The new password must meet the org's password policy. (Mutates live credentials.)

actions.name: Set Password
actions.description: Admin-sets a user's password directly, with no current password needed. Use this to reset the password for a user you manage. The new password must meet the org's password policy.

actions.name: Create Group
actions.description: Creates a new Okta group with a name and optional description. Use this to organize users for access assignment or rules.

actions.name: Get Group
actions.description: Retrieves a single group's details by id. Use this to read a group before updating it or managing its membership.

actions.name: List Groups
actions.description: Lists groups in your org, optionally narrowed by a quick search or a SCIM-style filter. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to find or enumerate groups.

actions.name: Update Group
actions.description: Replaces an Okta group's profile (name and description). Only OKTA_GROUP profiles can be edited - app and built-in groups cannot. Use this to rename or re-describe a group.

actions.name: Delete Group
actions.description: Permanently deletes an Okta group. Members are not deleted, only the group. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Add User to Group
actions.description: Adds a user to a group. Use this to grant the user whatever access the group confers.

actions.name: Remove User from Group
actions.description: Removes a user from a group, revoking any access the group conferred. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List Group Members
actions.description: Lists the users who are members of a group. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to audit who is in a group.

actions.name: List Assigned Apps for Group
actions.description: Lists the applications assigned to a group - every member of the group gets these apps. Use this to see what access a group grants.

actions.name: Create Group Rule
actions.description: Creates a group rule that auto-assigns matching users into one or more groups based on an Okta Expression Language condition. Created INACTIVE - activate it to start applying. Use this to automate group membership.

actions.name: List Group Rules
actions.description: Lists the group rules in your org. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to find a rule to activate, deactivate, or delete.

actions.name: Activate Group Rule
actions.description: Activates a group rule so it begins evaluating and assigning matching users. Use this after creating or editing a rule.

actions.name: Deactivate Group Rule
actions.description: Deactivates a group rule so it stops evaluating. A rule must be deactivated before it can be deleted. Use this to pause automated assignment.

actions.name: Delete Group Rule
actions.description: Deletes a group rule. The rule must be INACTIVE first (deactivate it before deleting). Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List Applications
actions.description: Lists the applications configured in your org, optionally narrowed by a quick search or filter. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to find an app to assign users or groups to.

actions.name: Get Application
actions.description: Retrieves a single application's details by id. Use this to inspect an app's sign-on mode and status before assigning users or groups.

actions.name: Assign User to Application
actions.description: Assigns a user to an application, optionally with an app-specific username. Use this to grant one person access to an app. (Requires a pre-configured app instance.)

actions.name: Unassign User from Application
actions.description: Removes a user's assignment to an application, revoking their access to it. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Assign Group to Application
actions.description: Assigns a group to an application - every member of the group gets the app. Optionally set an assignment priority. Use this to grant a whole team access at once. (Requires a pre-configured app instance.)

actions.name: Remove Group from Application
actions.description: Removes a group's assignment to an application, revoking the app for its members (unless granted another way). Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List Application Users
actions.description: Lists the users assigned to an application. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to audit who has access to an app.

actions.name: List Application Group Assignments
actions.description: Lists the group assignments for an application - which groups grant this app to their members. Use this to audit group-based access to an app.

actions.name: Assign Role to User
actions.description: Grants a standard Okta admin role to a user. Use this to delegate administrative privileges such as Help Desk or App Administrator.

actions.name: List Roles Assigned to User
actions.description: Lists the admin roles assigned to a user. Use this to audit a user's administrative privileges, or to find the assignment id needed to remove a role.

actions.name: Remove Role from User
actions.description: Removes an admin role from a user using the role ASSIGNMENT id (from List Roles Assigned to User - not the role type). Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List Factors
actions.description: Lists the MFA factors a user has enrolled (SMS, TOTP app, push, etc.) with their status. Use this to audit a user's multi-factor setup or to find a factor to activate or reset.

actions.name: Enroll Factor
actions.description: Enrolls a new MFA factor for a user (e.g. SMS, voice, email, or an authenticator app). SMS/voice/email enrollment sends a real message that the user confirms via Activate Factor. Use this to set up multi-factor for someone.

actions.name: Activate Factor
actions.description: Activates a PENDING_ACTIVATION factor using the one-time code the user received out-of-band (SMS/email/TOTP). Use this to finish enrolling a factor after Enroll Factor.

actions.name: Reset Factor
actions.description: Removes (unenrolls) an enrolled MFA factor for a user. Use this to clear a single factor - e.g. an old phone - without resetting all of the user's factors. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Get Logs
actions.description: Reads the org's System Log (audit trail) within a time window, optionally filtered by event type or a keyword. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to investigate activity or pull events for reporting.

actions.name: List Network Zones
actions.description: Lists the IP and dynamic network zones in your org, optionally narrowed by a SCIM filter. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to find a zone to view, edit, or use in a sign-on policy.

actions.name: Create Network Zone
actions.description: Creates a network zone - an IP zone (explicit CIDR/range list) or a dynamic zone (geo/ASN-based). Use a Policy zone in sign-on rules, or a Blocklist zone to always block its addresses.

actions.name: Get Network Zone
actions.description: Retrieves a single network zone by id, including its gateways and proxies. Use this to inspect a zone before editing it.

actions.name: Update Network Zone
actions.description: Replaces a network zone (PUT is a full replace - supply the name, type, and the complete set of gateways/proxies). Use this to edit a zone's address ranges or usage.

actions.name: Delete Network Zone
actions.description: Deletes a network zone. The zone must be INACTIVE first - deactivate it before deleting. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Network Zone
actions.description: Activates a network zone so it can be referenced in policies. Use this after creating or editing a zone.

actions.name: Deactivate Network Zone
actions.description: Deactivates a network zone (required before it can be deleted). Use this to take a zone out of service.

actions.name: List Trusted Origins
actions.description: Lists the trusted origins (CORS / redirect / iframe-embed) registered in your org. Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. Use this to find an origin to view or edit.

actions.name: Create Trusted Origin
actions.description: Registers a trusted origin and what it is trusted for (CORS, Redirect, and/or Iframe Embed). Use this to allow a web app at a specific URL to make CORS calls or be a redirect target.

actions.name: Get Trusted Origin
actions.description: Retrieves a single trusted origin by id. Use this to inspect an origin's scopes and status before editing it.

actions.name: Update Trusted Origin
actions.description: Replaces a trusted origin (PUT is a full replace - supply the name, origin, and the complete set of scopes). Use this to rename an origin or change what it is trusted for.

actions.name: Delete Trusted Origin
actions.description: Deletes a trusted origin, revoking the trust it conferred. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Trusted Origin
actions.description: Activates a trusted origin so its trust takes effect. Use this to re-enable a previously deactivated origin.

actions.name: Deactivate Trusted Origin
actions.description: Deactivates a trusted origin so its trust no longer applies. Use this to temporarily disable an origin without deleting it.

actions.name: List Event Hooks
actions.description: Lists the outbound event hooks in your org - registrations that POST Okta events to an external HTTPS endpoint. Use this to find a hook to view, edit, or activate.

actions.name: Create Event Hook
actions.description: Registers an event hook that POSTs the chosen Okta events to your HTTPS endpoint. After creating, verify the hook so Okta confirms your endpoint, then activate it. Use this to push Okta events to an external service.

actions.name: Get Event Hook
actions.description: Retrieves a single event hook by id, including its events and channel config. Use this to inspect a hook before editing or verifying it.

actions.name: Update Event Hook
actions.description: Replaces an event hook's name, events, and channel (full replace). Use this to change which events fire, the delivery URL, or the filter expression.

actions.name: Delete Event Hook
actions.description: Deletes an event hook. The hook must be INACTIVE first - deactivate it before deleting. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Event Hook
actions.description: Activates an event hook so Okta begins delivering its events. Use this after the hook is verified.

actions.name: Deactivate Event Hook
actions.description: Deactivates an event hook so Okta stops delivering its events (required before delete). Use this to pause a hook.

actions.name: Verify Event Hook
actions.description: Triggers Okta's one-time verification handshake against the hook's delivery URL. The endpoint must echo the X-Okta-Verification-Challenge header. Run this once after creating a hook, before activating it. (Requires a live external endpoint that answers the challenge.)

actions.name: List Inline Hooks
actions.description: Lists the inline hooks in your org - synchronous hooks Okta calls mid-flow (token transform, registration, password import, etc.). Optionally filter by type. Use this to find a hook to view or edit.

actions.name: Create Inline Hook
actions.description: Creates an inline hook - a synchronous call Okta makes to your HTTPS endpoint mid-flow that can modify the outcome. The type (which flow it plugs into) is immutable after creation. Use this to customize token, registration, or password-import behavior.

actions.name: Get Inline Hook
actions.description: Retrieves a single inline hook by id, including its type and channel config. Use this to inspect a hook before editing it.

actions.name: Update Inline Hook
actions.description: Updates an inline hook's name and channel (the type is immutable). Use this to change the endpoint URL, auth, or display name of a hook.

actions.name: Delete Inline Hook
actions.description: Deletes an inline hook. The hook must be INACTIVE first - deactivate it before deleting. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Inline Hook
actions.description: Activates an inline hook so Okta begins calling it during the relevant flow. Use this after creating or editing a hook.

actions.name: Deactivate Inline Hook
actions.description: Deactivates an inline hook so Okta stops calling it (required before delete). Use this to pause a hook.

actions.name: Execute Inline Hook
actions.description: Test-fires an inline hook against your endpoint with a sample CloudEvents payload, returning the commands your endpoint responds with. Use this to validate a hook end to end. (Requires a live external endpoint reachable from Okta.)

actions.name: Create Application
actions.description: Creates an app instance in your org. Bookmark = a clickable link tile on the dashboard; SWA Auto-Login = a form-fill SSO app. The settings fields change with the chosen sign-on mode. (OIDC/SAML apps are configured in the Admin Console, not here.)

actions.name: Replace Application
actions.description: Replaces an app instance (PUT is a full replace - supply the label, sign-on mode and the complete settings). Use this to edit a Bookmark or SWA app's URLs.

actions.name: Delete Application
actions.description: Permanently deletes an app instance. The app must be INACTIVE first - deactivate it before deleting. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Application
actions.description: Activates an app instance so it appears for assigned users. Use this after creating or editing an app.

actions.name: Deactivate Application
actions.description: Deactivates an app instance (required before it can be deleted). Use this to take an app out of service.

actions.name: List Behavior Rules
actions.description: Lists the behavior detection rules in your org (velocity / impossible-travel, anomalous location / IP / device / ASN). Use this to find a rule to view, edit, or use in a risk-based sign-on policy.

actions.name: Create Behavior Rule
actions.description: Creates a behavior detection rule. Pick the anomaly type (e.g. Velocity for impossible travel) and the tuning fields adjust to it. Reference the rule in a sign-on policy to trigger step-up MFA on suspicious behavior.

actions.name: Get Behavior Rule
actions.description: Retrieves a single behavior detection rule by id, including its type and tuning settings. Use this to inspect a rule before editing it.

actions.name: Update Behavior Rule
actions.description: Replaces a behavior detection rule (PUT is a full replace - supply name, type and the complete settings). Use this to retune a rule's thresholds.

actions.name: Delete Behavior Rule
actions.description: Deletes a behavior detection rule. Destructive - once removed it can no longer be referenced by policies.

actions.name: Activate Behavior Rule
actions.description: Activates a behavior detection rule so it starts evaluating sign-ins. Use this after creating or retuning a rule.

actions.name: Deactivate Behavior Rule
actions.description: Deactivates a behavior detection rule so it stops evaluating sign-ins. Use this to pause a rule without deleting it.

actions.name: List User Types
actions.description: Lists the user types in your org (the default type plus any custom types). Each type can carry its own profile schema. Use this to find a type to assign at user creation or to edit.

actions.name: Create User Type
actions.description: Creates a custom user type. The machine name is immutable after creation; only the display name and description can change later. An org allows the default type plus up to 9 custom types.

actions.name: Get User Type
actions.description: Retrieves a single user type by id. Pick a type from the dropdown, or use the org default. Use this to inspect a type before editing.

actions.name: Update User Type
actions.description: Partially updates a user type - only the display name and description are mutable (the machine name is fixed). Leave a field blank to keep its current value.

actions.name: Replace User Type
actions.description: Fully replaces a user type. PUT requires the machine name echoed back (it stays immutable - it must equal the existing name) plus the new display name and description.

actions.name: Delete User Type
actions.description: Deletes a custom user type. You cannot delete the default type or a type that is currently assigned to users. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Get ThreatInsight Configuration
actions.description: Retrieves your org's ThreatInsight configuration - how Okta responds to sign-ins from suspicious IPs and which network zones are excluded. Read this before updating it.

actions.name: Update ThreatInsight Configuration
actions.description: Sets how Okta responds to sign-ins from suspicious IPs (None / Audit / Block) and optionally excludes trusted network zones from ThreatInsight. Read the current config first, then write your change.

actions.name: List Devices
actions.description: Lists the managed/enrolled devices in your org, optionally narrowed by a SCIM filter (e.g. status eq "ACTIVE" or profile.platform eq "WINDOWS"). Returns a page plus a cursor for the next page. (Devices enroll via Okta Verify / client agents - they are not created through this API.)

actions.name: Get Device
actions.description: Retrieves a single device by id, including its profile (display name, platform, OS, encryption status). Use this to inspect a device before acting on it.

actions.name: Delete Device
actions.description: Permanently deletes a device. The device must be DEACTIVATED first - deactivate it before deleting. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Device
actions.description: Activates a device, returning it to the ACTIVE state. Use this to bring a deactivated or new device back into service.

actions.name: Deactivate Device
actions.description: Deactivates a device (required before it can be deleted). This is destructive to the device's factors and certificates. Use with care.

actions.name: Suspend Device
actions.description: Temporarily suspends a device (non-destructive). A suspended device cannot be used for sign-in until unsuspended.

actions.name: Unsuspend Device
actions.description: Unsuspends a previously suspended device, returning it to ACTIVE. Only SUSPENDED devices can be unsuspended.

actions.name: List Device Users
actions.description: Lists the users linked to a device, with each user's management status and screen-lock type. Use this to see who a device belongs to.

actions.name: List User Blocks
actions.description: Lists the IP/device blocks currently applied to a user (e.g. from a suspicious-activity event). Use this to see why a user may be locked out from certain locations or devices.

actions.name: List User Devices
actions.description: Lists the devices enrolled or managed for a user, with each device's status and platform. Use this to audit which devices a user signs in from.

actions.name: Reactivate User
actions.description: Restarts the activation flow for a user in the PROVISIONED or DEPROVISIONED state. With "Send Email" off, returns a fresh activation URL/token you can deliver yourself. Use this to re-invite a staged or deactivated user.

actions.name: Forgot Password
actions.description: Starts the self-service password reset flow for a user. With "Send Email" off, returns a one-time reset URL you can deliver yourself instead of emailing it. Use this to help a user who is locked out.

actions.name: Reset Password via Recovery Question
actions.description: Resets a user's password by answering their existing recovery question. Use this for a self-service reset where the user knows their recovery answer but not their password.

actions.name: Change Recovery Question
actions.description: Changes a user's recovery question and answer, validating the change against their current password. Use this to update the question a user answers during self-service password recovery.

actions.name: Verify Factor
actions.description: Verifies an enrolled MFA factor by submitting its passcode (sms/call/email/totp/token) or its security-question answer. Leave the passcode empty on the first call to trigger a CHALLENGE that sends the code. Use this to complete a step-up verification.

actions.name: Resend Enrollment Challenge
actions.description: Re-sends the activation challenge for a factor that is still PENDING_ACTIVATION (e.g. resend the SMS or email code). Use this when the original code expired or never arrived.

actions.name: List Supported Factors
actions.description: Lists the MFA factor types a user is eligible to enroll, filtered by your org's policy. Use this to show a user only the factors they can actually set up.

actions.name: List Security Questions
actions.description: Lists the security questions available for a user to enroll as a recovery/MFA factor. Use this to populate a question picker before enrolling the security-question factor.

actions.name: Get Group Rule
actions.description: Retrieves a single group rule by id - its match expression, target groups, exclusions, and status. Turn on "Expand Group Names" to embed a group-id->name map so the result is easier to read.

actions.name: Replace Group Rule
actions.description: Fully replaces a group rule's name, match expression, target groups, and exclusions. The rule must be INACTIVE first (deactivate it before replacing). Use this to edit how an auto-assignment rule works.

actions.name: List Group Owners
actions.description: Lists the owners assigned to a group - the users or groups that can manage its membership. Use this to audit who controls a group.

actions.name: Assign Group Owner
actions.description: Assigns a user or group as an owner of a group, letting them manage its membership. Pick the owner type, then paste the user or group ID. Use this to delegate group administration.

actions.name: Remove Group Owner
actions.description: Removes an owner from a group, revoking their ability to manage its membership. Use the owner id from List Group Owners. Destructive.

actions.name: List Linked Object Definitions
actions.description: Lists the custom user-to-user relationship definitions in your org (e.g. manager/subordinate, mentor/mentee). Use this to see which linked-object relationships exist before linking users.

actions.name: Create Linked Object Definition
actions.description: Creates a custom user-to-user relationship definition with a primary side and an associated side (e.g. manager / subordinate). Use this once to define a relationship you can then assign between users.

actions.name: Get Linked Object Definition
actions.description: Retrieves a single linked-object definition by its primary or associated name. Use this to inspect a relationship's two sides before linking users.

actions.name: Delete Linked Object Definition
actions.description: Deletes a whole linked-object relationship definition (both sides). Specify either the primary or associated name. Destructive - any user links using this relationship are removed.

actions.name: Link Users
actions.description: Links two users in a relationship: the "Associated User" becomes the associated side (e.g. the subordinate) and the "Primary User" becomes the primary side (e.g. the manager) for the chosen relationship. Use this to set a manager, mentor, or other custom link.

actions.name: List User Linked Values
actions.description: Lists a user's linked values for a relationship - the link to their primary (if they are associated) or their associated users (if they are primary). Use this to read who a user reports to or who reports to them.

actions.name: Unlink User
actions.description: Removes a user's relationship link for the named relationship (clears the link on the associated user's side). Use this to unset a manager, mentor, or other custom link. Destructive.

actions.name: List Policies
actions.description: Lists policies of a given type (e.g. Global Session, Password, MFA Enroll). Okta requires the policy type - pick it from the dropdown. Use this to find a policy id before editing it or its rules.

actions.name: Create Policy
actions.description: Creates a policy of the chosen type. OKTA_SIGN_ON (Global Session) and PASSWORD work on any org; ACCESS_POLICY / PROFILE_ENROLLMENT require an Identity Engine org. Conditions and actions are type-specific sub-forms.

actions.name: Get Policy
actions.description: Retrieves a single policy by id, including its conditions. Use this to inspect a policy before editing it or its rules.

actions.name: Replace Policy
actions.description: Fully replaces a policy (PUT overwrite). The type is immutable and must match the existing policy. Use this to rename a policy or change its conditions/actions.

actions.name: Delete Policy
actions.description: Deletes a policy permanently. Destructive - its rules go with it. Use with care.

actions.name: Activate Policy
actions.description: Activates a policy so it is evaluated at sign-in. Use this after creating or editing a policy.

actions.name: Deactivate Policy
actions.description: Deactivates a policy (required before it can be deleted in some cases). Use this to take a policy out of evaluation without deleting it.

actions.name: List Policy Rules
actions.description: Lists the rules inside a policy, in evaluation order. Use this to find a rule id before editing or reordering it.

actions.name: Create Policy Rule
actions.description: Adds a rule to a policy. The rule type must match the parent policy (e.g. SIGN_ON for a Global Session policy). Conditions and actions are type-specific sub-forms.

actions.name: Get Policy Rule
actions.description: Retrieves a single rule inside a policy by id. Use this to inspect a rule's conditions and actions before editing.

actions.name: Replace Policy Rule
actions.description: Fully replaces a rule inside a policy (PUT overwrite). The rule type is immutable and must match the existing rule. Use this to change a rule's conditions or actions.

actions.name: Delete Policy Rule
actions.description: Deletes a rule from a policy permanently. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Policy Rule
actions.description: Activates a rule inside a policy so it is evaluated. Use this after creating or editing a rule.

actions.name: Deactivate Policy Rule
actions.description: Deactivates a rule inside a policy (required before it can be deleted). Use this to disable a rule without deleting it.

actions.name: List Authorization Servers
actions.description: Lists custom OAuth 2.0 / OIDC authorization servers in your org. Use this to find a server id before managing its scopes, claims, or lifecycle. Requires the API Access Management add-on.

actions.name: Create Authorization Server
actions.description: Creates a custom OAuth 2.0 / OIDC authorization server with one or more token audiences. Use this to issue API access tokens with custom scopes and claims. Requires the API Access Management add-on.

actions.name: Get Authorization Server
actions.description: Retrieves a single authorization server by id, including its audiences, issuer, and signing credentials. Use this to inspect a server before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Authorization Server
actions.description: Fully replaces an authorization server (PUT overwrite - supply name and the complete audience list). Use this to rename a server or change its audiences/issuer mode.

actions.name: Delete Authorization Server
actions.description: Deletes an authorization server permanently. It must be deactivated first (Okta returns 403 if it is active). Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Authorization Server
actions.description: Activates an authorization server so it can issue tokens. Use this after creating or editing a server.

actions.name: Deactivate Authorization Server
actions.description: Deactivates an authorization server (required before it can be deleted). Use this to stop a server from issuing tokens without deleting it.

actions.name: List Token Scopes
actions.description: Lists the custom OAuth scopes defined on an authorization server. Use this to find a scope id before editing or deleting it.

actions.name: Create Token Scope
actions.description: Creates a custom OAuth scope on an authorization server. Use this to define a permission (e.g. car:drive) that apps can request. Requires the API Access Management add-on.

actions.name: Get Token Scope
actions.description: Retrieves a single custom OAuth scope by id. Use this to inspect a scope before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Token Scope
actions.description: Fully replaces a custom OAuth scope (PUT overwrite). Use this to rename a scope or change its consent behavior.

actions.name: Delete Token Scope
actions.description: Deletes a custom OAuth scope from an authorization server permanently. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List Token Claims
actions.description: Lists the custom token claims defined on an authorization server. Use this to find a claim id before editing or deleting it.

actions.name: Create Token Claim
actions.description: Creates a custom token claim on an authorization server - a value (Okta Expression or a group match) injected into the access or ID token. Requires the API Access Management add-on.

actions.name: Get Token Claim
actions.description: Retrieves a single custom token claim by id. Use this to inspect a claim before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Token Claim
actions.description: Fully replaces a custom token claim (PUT overwrite). Use this to change a claim's value, type, or scope binding.

actions.name: Delete Token Claim
actions.description: Deletes a custom token claim from an authorization server permanently. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Get User Schema
actions.description: Retrieves a user profile schema (base + custom attributes). Use 'default' for the default user type, or a type's schema id from List User Types. Use this to inspect the available profile attributes before mapping or updating them.

actions.name: Update User Schema
actions.description: Adds, edits, or removes custom user profile attributes by sending a JSON-Schema Draft 4 fragment. Use this to extend the user profile with org-specific fields. Set a property to null to remove it.

actions.name: Get Group Schema
actions.description: Retrieves the group profile schema (base + custom attributes). Use this to inspect the available group profile attributes.

actions.name: Update Group Schema
actions.description: Adds, edits, or removes custom group profile attributes via a JSON-Schema fragment. Base group properties cannot be changed. Set a property to null to remove it.

actions.name: Get App User Schema
actions.description: Retrieves an application's user profile schema (the attributes Okta provisions to that app). Use this to inspect app-user attributes before editing mappings.

actions.name: Update App User Schema
actions.description: Adds, edits, or removes custom attributes on an application's user schema via a JSON-Schema fragment. App custom properties may carry an externalName. Set a property to null to remove it.

actions.name: List Profile Mappings
actions.description: Lists the attribute mappings between sources and targets (e.g. Okta user -> an app's user profile). Use this to find a mapping id before retrieving or editing it.

actions.name: Get Profile Mapping
actions.description: Retrieves a single profile mapping by id, including each target property's expression and push status. Use this to inspect a mapping before editing it.

actions.name: Update Profile Mapping
actions.description: Updates the attribute mappings on a profile mapping. Each target property maps to {expression, pushStatus}. Use this to control which attributes flow to a downstream app. Set a property to null to remove its mapping.

actions.name: Get Session
actions.description: Retrieves a single sign-in session by its id, including the user, status, and expiry. Use this to inspect a live session (the id comes from a prior authentication or a System Log event). There is no list-sessions endpoint, so the id is entered directly.

actions.name: Refresh Session
actions.description: Extends a sign-in session's lifetime (its expiry is pushed out). Use this to keep an active session alive.

actions.name: Revoke Session
actions.description: Immediately ends a single sign-in session - the user is signed out of it. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Revoke All User Sessions
actions.description: Ends all of a user's sign-in sessions, forcing them to re-authenticate everywhere. Optionally also revoke their OAuth tokens and clear remembered devices. Use this to lock out a compromised account.

actions.name: List Authenticators
actions.description: Lists the MFA authenticators configured in your org (email, phone, security key, etc.) with each one's status. Use this to find an authenticator id before editing it.

actions.name: Create Authenticator
actions.description: Adds an MFA authenticator to your org (e.g. Duo Security or a Temporary Access Code). Fill in the connection settings for the chosen type. Use this to offer a new sign-in factor.

actions.name: Get Authenticator
actions.description: Retrieves a single authenticator by id, including its settings and status. Use this to inspect an authenticator before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Authenticator
actions.description: Fully replaces an authenticator's configuration (PUT overwrite). The type must match the existing one. Built-in authenticators (email/password/phone/security question) are configured this way.

actions.name: Activate Authenticator
actions.description: Activates an authenticator so users can enroll and use it for MFA. Use this after adding or configuring one.

actions.name: Deactivate Authenticator
actions.description: Deactivates an authenticator so users can no longer enroll or use it for MFA. Use with care - existing enrollments may be affected.

actions.name: List Authenticator Methods
actions.description: Lists the methods within an authenticator (e.g. SMS and voice under the Phone authenticator) with each method's status. Use this to find a method type before editing it.

actions.name: Get Authenticator Method
actions.description: Retrieves a single method within an authenticator, including its settings. Use this to inspect a method before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Authenticator Method
actions.description: Updates a method within an authenticator (its status and any method-specific settings, e.g. WebAuthn user verification). Use this to tune how a method behaves.

actions.name: Activate Authenticator Method
actions.description: Activates a method within an authenticator (e.g. enable SMS under Phone). Use this to let users enroll that method.

actions.name: Deactivate Authenticator Method
actions.description: Deactivates a method within an authenticator (e.g. disable voice under Phone). Use with care.

actions.name: List Custom AAGUIDs
actions.description: Lists the custom WebAuthn AAGUIDs (security-key vendor/model allowlist) on an authenticator. Use this to see which security-key models are permitted.

actions.name: Create Custom AAGUID
actions.description: Adds a custom WebAuthn AAGUID (a security-key vendor/model) to an authenticator's allowlist, with its attestation root certificate(s). Use this to permit a specific security-key model.

actions.name: Get Custom AAGUID
actions.description: Retrieves a single custom WebAuthn AAGUID by id. Use this to inspect an allowed security-key model.

actions.name: Replace Custom AAGUID
actions.description: Fully replaces a custom WebAuthn AAGUID (PUT overwrite). Use this to rename it or update its attestation certificates.

actions.name: Delete Custom AAGUID
actions.description: Removes a custom WebAuthn AAGUID from an authenticator's allowlist. That security-key model is no longer permitted. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Get Well-Known App Authenticator Config
actions.description: Retrieves the public discovery document for a custom-app (push) authenticator - its enroll endpoint and supported methods - for a given OAuth client id. Read-only.

actions.name: List Identity Providers
actions.description: Lists the external identity providers (social and enterprise IdPs) configured in your org. Use this to find an IdP id before editing, activating, or deleting it.

actions.name: Create Identity Provider
actions.description: Adds an external identity provider so users can sign in through it (e.g. Google, a generic OIDC, or SAML 2.0 provider). Fill in the connection settings for the chosen type. Use this to enable social or enterprise federated sign-in.

actions.name: Get Identity Provider
actions.description: Retrieves a single identity provider by id, including its protocol and policy configuration. Use this to inspect an IdP before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Identity Provider
actions.description: Fully replaces an identity provider's configuration (PUT overwrite). The type must match the existing IdP. Use this to update connection settings or policy.

actions.name: Delete Identity Provider
actions.description: Deletes an identity provider permanently. Deactivate it first. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Identity Provider
actions.description: Activates an identity provider so users can sign in through it. Use this after creating or reconfiguring an IdP.

actions.name: Deactivate Identity Provider
actions.description: Deactivates an identity provider so users can no longer sign in through it. Required before deleting.

actions.name: List IdP Users
actions.description: Lists the Okta users linked to an identity provider. Use this to audit which users authenticate through an external IdP.

actions.name: Get IdP User
actions.description: Retrieves a single IdP-linked user, including the external id mapping. Use this to inspect a user's link to an external IdP.

actions.name: Link User to IdP
actions.description: Links an Okta user to an external identity provider by external id, so they can sign in through it. Use this for SAML/social IdPs that map by a known subject id.

actions.name: Unlink User from IdP
actions.description: Removes the link between an Okta user and an external identity provider. The user can no longer sign in through that IdP. Use with care.

actions.name: List User Grants
actions.description: Lists the OAuth 2.0 consent grants a user has approved (the scopes they have allowed apps to access). Use this to audit a user's consents before revoking them.

actions.name: Revoke All User Grants
actions.description: Revokes ALL OAuth 2.0 consent grants for a user - every app loses the access the user previously approved. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Get User Grant
actions.description: Retrieves a single OAuth consent grant by id. Use this to inspect a grant before revoking it.

actions.name: Revoke User Grant
actions.description: Revokes a single OAuth consent grant - the app loses access to that one scope. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List User Clients
actions.description: Lists the OAuth clients (apps) that currently hold grants or refresh tokens for a user. Use this to find a client id before listing or revoking its grants/tokens.

actions.name: List Grants For Client
actions.description: Lists the OAuth consent grants a user gave to one specific client (app). Use this to scope an audit or revoke to a single app.

actions.name: Revoke Grants For Client
actions.description: Revokes all OAuth consent grants a user gave to one specific client (app). Destructive - that app loses all the user's approved scopes.

actions.name: List Refresh Tokens For Client
actions.description: Lists the OAuth refresh tokens one client (app) holds for a user. Use this to find a token id before getting or revoking it.

actions.name: Revoke All Tokens For Client
actions.description: Revokes all OAuth refresh tokens one client (app) holds for a user - the user must re-authenticate to that app. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Get Refresh Token
actions.description: Retrieves a single OAuth refresh token by id. Use this to inspect a token before revoking it.

actions.name: Revoke Refresh Token
actions.description: Revokes a single OAuth refresh token - the user must re-authenticate to that app for that session. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List Authorization Server Policies
actions.description: Lists the access policies on a custom authorization server. Use this to find a policy id before editing its rules. Requires the API Access Management add-on.

actions.name: Create Authorization Server Policy
actions.description: Creates an OAuth access policy on a custom authorization server. By default it applies to all clients; pass a Conditions object to scope it to specific client ids. Requires the API Access Management add-on.

actions.name: Get Authorization Server Policy
actions.description: Retrieves a single access policy from a custom authorization server. Use this to inspect a policy before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Authorization Server Policy
actions.description: Fully replaces an access policy on a custom authorization server (PUT overwrite). Use this to rename a policy or change its client scope.

actions.name: Delete Authorization Server Policy
actions.description: Deletes an access policy from a custom authorization server permanently. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Authorization Server Policy
actions.description: Activates an access policy on a custom authorization server so it starts enforcing. Use this after creating a staged policy.

actions.name: Deactivate Authorization Server Policy
actions.description: Deactivates an access policy on a custom authorization server (required before delete). Use this to take a policy out of service.

actions.name: List Authorization Server Policy Rules
actions.description: Lists the rules of an access policy on a custom authorization server. Use this to find a rule id before editing it.

actions.name: Create Authorization Server Policy Rule
actions.description: Creates a rule within an access policy on a custom authorization server - it decides which people, grant types, and scopes the policy permits, and sets the token lifetimes. Requires the API Access Management add-on.

actions.name: Get Authorization Server Policy Rule
actions.description: Retrieves a single rule of an access policy on a custom authorization server. Use this to inspect a rule before editing it.

actions.name: Replace Authorization Server Policy Rule
actions.description: Fully replaces a rule of an access policy on a custom authorization server (PUT overwrite). Use this to change which people/grant types/scopes the rule permits or its token lifetimes.

actions.name: Delete Authorization Server Policy Rule
actions.description: Deletes a rule from an access policy on a custom authorization server permanently. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Authorization Server Policy Rule
actions.description: Activates a rule within an access policy on a custom authorization server so it starts enforcing.

actions.name: Deactivate Authorization Server Policy Rule
actions.description: Deactivates a rule within an access policy on a custom authorization server (required before delete).

actions.name: List Authorization Server Keys
actions.description: Lists the signing keys (JWKs) of a custom authorization server - Okta uses these to sign tokens. Use this to read the current and next keys.

actions.name: Get Authorization Server Key
actions.description: Retrieves a single signing key (JWK) of a custom authorization server by its kid. Use this to inspect a specific key.

actions.name: Rotate Authorization Server Keys
actions.description: Rotates the signing keys of a custom authorization server - the NEXT key becomes ACTIVE and a fresh NEXT key is generated. Use this for scheduled key rotation. Only signing keys are rotatable.

actions.name: List Authorization Server Clients
actions.description: Lists the OAuth 2.0 client applications that use a custom authorization server. Use this to audit which apps request tokens from this server.

actions.name: Get Application User
actions.description: Retrieves a user's assignment to an application, including their app-specific profile and credentials. Use this to inspect what a user looks like inside an app.

actions.name: Update Application User
actions.description: Updates a user's assignment to an application - set their app-specific username/password (for SWA/password-push apps) and/or their app profile attributes. Use this to fix a user's identity inside an app.

actions.name: Get Application Group Assignment
actions.description: Retrieves a group's assignment to an application, including the assignment profile (e.g. mapped manager/department). Use this to inspect how a group provisions to an app.

actions.name: Update Application Group Assignment
actions.description: Updates a group's assignment-profile on an application via JSON-Patch operations - e.g. change the manager or department mapped to that group. Use this to adjust how a group provisions without re-assigning it.

actions.name: List Application Keys
actions.description: Lists an application's SSO signing-key credentials (the X.509 certificates Okta uses to sign SAML assertions for this app). Use this to find a key id before getting or cloning it.

actions.name: Generate Application Key
actions.description: Generates a new SSO signing-key credential (X.509 certificate) for an application, valid for the chosen number of years. Use this to add a fresh signing cert ahead of rotation.

actions.name: Get Application Key
actions.description: Retrieves a single SSO signing-key credential of an application by its kid. Use this to inspect a specific certificate.

actions.name: Clone Application Key
actions.description: Copies an application's SSO signing-key credential into another application. Use this when two apps must share the same signing certificate.

actions.name: List Application CSRs
actions.description: Lists an application's certificate signing requests (CSRs). Use this to find a CSR id before getting, revoking, or publishing a signed cert against it.

actions.name: Generate Application CSR
actions.description: Generates a certificate signing request (CSR) for an application so you can have it signed by your own CA, then publish the signed cert back. Provide the certificate subject (at least a common name).

actions.name: Get Application CSR
actions.description: Retrieves a single certificate signing request (CSR) of an application by its id. Use this to read the CSR text to send to your CA.

actions.name: Revoke Application CSR
actions.description: Revokes (deletes) a pending certificate signing request from an application. Use this to discard a CSR you no longer intend to publish. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Publish Application CSR
actions.description: Publishes a CA-signed certificate against an application's pending CSR, turning it into an active signing-key credential. Paste the signed certificate in PEM format.

actions.name: List Application Client Keys
actions.description: Lists the OAuth/OIDC client JSON Web Keys (JWKs) registered on an application (for private_key_jwt client authentication). Use this to find a JWK id before getting or managing it.

actions.name: Add Application Client Key
actions.description: Adds an OAuth/OIDC client JSON Web Key (JWK) to an application - the public half of a key the client uses for private_key_jwt authentication. Supply the RSA public key (kid, e, n).

actions.name: Get Application Client Key
actions.description: Retrieves a single OAuth/OIDC client JSON Web Key of an application by its id. Use this to inspect a registered client key.

actions.name: Delete Application Client Key
actions.description: Deletes an inactive OAuth/OIDC client JSON Web Key from an application. The key must be deactivated first. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Application Client Key
actions.description: Activates an OAuth/OIDC client JSON Web Key on an application so the client can authenticate with it.

actions.name: Deactivate Application Client Key
actions.description: Deactivates an OAuth/OIDC client JSON Web Key on an application (required before delete).

actions.name: List Application Client Secrets
actions.description: Lists the OAuth/OIDC client secrets registered on an application. The full secret value is only returned at creation; this shows ids, status, and hashes. Use this to find a secret id.

actions.name: Create Application Client Secret
actions.description: Creates a new OAuth/OIDC client secret on an application. Leave the secret blank to let Okta generate one - the full value is returned only this once, so capture it. Use this to rotate a client secret.

actions.name: Get Application Client Secret
actions.description: Retrieves a single OAuth/OIDC client secret of an application by its id. The full secret value is only shown at creation; later reads may mask it.

actions.name: Delete Application Client Secret
actions.description: Deletes an inactive OAuth/OIDC client secret from an application. The secret must be deactivated first. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Application Client Secret
actions.description: Activates an OAuth/OIDC client secret on an application so it can be used to authenticate the client.

actions.name: Deactivate Application Client Secret
actions.description: Deactivates an OAuth/OIDC client secret on an application (required before delete).

actions.name: Upload Application Logo
actions.description: Uploads a logo image for an application (PNG/JPG/SVG/GIF, under 1 MB; 200x200 recommended). Pick a file from Flowrunner storage. The image is streamed to Okta as a multipart upload.

actions.name: List Resource Server Keys
actions.description: Lists the resource-server public JSON Web Keys of a custom authorization server (used to validate encrypted/signed requests). Use this to find a key id before managing it.

actions.name: Add Resource Server Key
actions.description: Adds a resource-server public JSON Web Key to a custom authorization server. Supply the RSA public key (kid, e, n) and whether it's for signing or encryption.

actions.name: Get Resource Server Key
actions.description: Retrieves a single resource-server JSON Web Key of a custom authorization server by its id.

actions.name: Delete Resource Server Key
actions.description: Deletes an inactive resource-server JSON Web Key from a custom authorization server. The key must be deactivated first. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Activate Resource Server Key
actions.description: Activates a resource-server JSON Web Key on a custom authorization server so it can be used.

actions.name: Deactivate Resource Server Key
actions.description: Deactivates a resource-server JSON Web Key on a custom authorization server (required before delete).

actions.name: List Org IdP Keys
actions.description: Lists the org-level Identity Provider key store - X.509 certificates you upload once and reference from multiple IdP configs. Paginates through every shared cert. Use this to find a key id.

actions.name: Create Org IdP Key
actions.description: Adds an X.509 certificate to the org-level Identity Provider key store, so IdP configs can reference it. Paste the certificate as base64-DER (a PEM body with no header lines).

actions.name: Get Org IdP Key
actions.description: Retrieves a single org-level Identity Provider key by its kid. Use this to inspect a shared certificate.

actions.name: Replace Org IdP Key
actions.description: Fully replaces an org-level Identity Provider key (PUT overwrite) with a new X.509 certificate. Use this to rotate a shared certificate.

actions.name: Delete Org IdP Key
actions.description: Deletes an org-level Identity Provider key from the key store. The key must not be in use by any IdP. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: List IdP Signing Keys
actions.description: Lists the signing keys of a specific Identity Provider - the certificates Okta uses to sign requests to that IdP. Use this to find a key id before getting or cloning it.

actions.name: List Active IdP Signing Key
actions.description: Retrieves the currently active signing key(s) of a specific Identity Provider. Use this to see which cert Okta is signing requests with right now.

actions.name: Generate IdP Signing Key
actions.description: Generates a new signing key (X.509 certificate) for a specific Identity Provider, valid for the chosen number of years. Use this to add a fresh signing cert ahead of rotation.

actions.name: Get IdP Signing Key
actions.description: Retrieves a single signing key of a specific Identity Provider by its kid. Use this to inspect a certificate.

actions.name: Clone IdP Signing Key
actions.description: Copies an Identity Provider's signing key into another IdP. Use this when two IdP configs must share the same signing certificate.

actions.name: List IdP CSRs
actions.description: Lists the certificate signing requests (CSRs) of a specific Identity Provider. Use this to find a CSR id before getting, revoking, or publishing a signed cert against it.

actions.name: Generate IdP CSR
actions.description: Generates a certificate signing request (CSR) for a specific Identity Provider so you can have it signed by your own CA, then publish the signed cert back. Provide the certificate subject (at least a common name).

actions.name: Get IdP CSR
actions.description: Retrieves a single certificate signing request (CSR) of a specific Identity Provider by its id. Use this to read the CSR text to send to your CA.

actions.name: Revoke IdP CSR
actions.description: Revokes (deletes) a pending certificate signing request from a specific Identity Provider. Use this to discard a CSR you no longer intend to publish. Destructive - use with care.

actions.name: Publish IdP CSR
actions.description: Publishes a CA-signed certificate against a specific Identity Provider's pending CSR, turning it into an active signing key. Paste the signed certificate in PEM format.

actions.name: List Social IdP User Tokens
actions.description: Lists the social/OIDC tokens Okta holds for a user linked through a specific Identity Provider (e.g. the access/ID tokens from the upstream provider). Use this to inspect a linked user's stored tokens.

idealUseCases: Onboard and offboard employees: create, activate, suspend, deactivate, or delete users.
Keep group membership current - add/remove users, or let group rules auto-assign by attribute.
Grant or revoke application access for a user or a whole group.
Delegate administration by assigning and removing standard admin roles.
Reset passwords and MFA factors for help-desk and security workflows, including recovery-question and verify/resend flows.
Define custom user-to-user relationships (e.g. manager/subordinate, mentor/mentee) with linked objects, and assign group owners.
Pull System Log events for auditing, alerting, or reporting, or react in real time via the polling trigger.
Define IP/dynamic network zones and CORS/redirect trusted origins for sign-on policy and app security.
Register outbound event hooks to push Okta events to your services, and inline hooks to customize token, registration, and password-import flows.
Connect external identity providers (OIDC, SAML 2.0, social) and link or unlink users to them.
Extend user, group, and app profile schemas with custom attributes, and tune profile mappings to downstream apps.
Add and configure MFA authenticators (Duo, Temporary Access Code, WebAuthn allowlists) and their methods.
Audit and revoke a user's OAuth grants and refresh tokens, and clear a user's sign-in sessions in response to a security event.
Rotate application and identity-provider credentials: generate or clone SSO signing keys, manage CSRs, register OAuth client keys/secrets, and rotate authorization-server signing keys.
Govern API access on custom authorization servers by managing access policies, their rules, and resource-server keys.
agentIdeas: **Automated onboarding**: create a user, add them to the right groups, assign their applications, and enroll an MFA factor - all in one flow triggered by a new-hire record.
**Security-incident response**: watch the System Log trigger for a suspicious event, then suspend the user, revoke their OAuth grants and refresh tokens, and clear their active sessions.
**Access recertification**: list a user's admin roles, application assignments, and OAuth grants on a schedule and report anything unexpected for review.
**Self-service password/MFA help desk**: reset a password or MFA factors, resend an enrollment challenge, or unlock a locked-out user from a support ticket.

**Policy-as-code**: create sign-on/password policies with their rules, define network zones and trusted origins, and toggle ThreatInsight to enforce security posture from version-controlled definitions.
**Credential rotation**: rotate authorization-server signing keys, generate or clone application SSO keys, and manage CSRs and OAuth client secrets on a recurring routine.
**IdP lifecycle**: connect an external OIDC/SAML identity provider, link users to it, and manage its signing keys and CSRs.
**Group-driven automation**: define group rules that auto-assign users by profile attribute, then grant those groups application access.
